$comment: PACT attack scenario catalogue. Companion to spec/PACT_v1.md §18.8.

field_definitions.title: Human-readable title.
field_definitions.description: What the attack does, in narrative form.
field_definitions.lemma_ref: Reference to a formal lemma in spec/models/ (or 'documented_limitation' / 'informative').
field_definitions.test_ref: Reference to a Stage 2 probe path or formal model file.
field_definitions.predicted_error: The fault.code value (per spec §18.3) the substrate is expected to emit when rejecting this attack. May be 'pact_ok' if the substrate is expected to accept (e.g., key-compromise attacks).
field_definitions.predicted_status: HTTP status when PACT is carried over HTTP. 200 = accept, 401/403/410 per §18.3 mapping.
field_definitions.version_introduced: Spec version when this scenario was added.
field_definitions.v0_7_behavior: How v0.7 implementations handle this scenario.
field_definitions.v0_8_behavior: How v0.8 implementations handle this scenario.

scenarios.title: Delegation block tries to grant more than parent has
scenarios.description: An attenuated cap-chain link attempts to grant an action or remove a caveat that was not in the parent's grant. The chain-walk re-derivation (§16.2) should detect this and reject.
scenarios.notes: Verified inductively by Tamarin P_MONO + P_MONO_transitive lemmas. Empirical evidence: D4 Phase A BASELINE C9 pass.

scenarios.title: Chain length exceeds max_depth caveat
scenarios.description: A delegation chain is longer than the max_depth caveat on the root cap. The verifier should reject by counting chain links.
scenarios.notes: max_depth is a Simple-profile caveat (§18.4.1). Empirical: D4 BASELINE pass.

scenarios.title: REQ replayed after audit_context.expires_at
scenarios.description: An attacker captures a legitimate REQ and replays it after the audit_context.expires_at timestamp has passed. The receiver should reject based on the expiry check.
scenarios.v0_8_behavior: rejected via audit_context.expires_at and deadline (both checked)
scenarios.notes: Verified by Tamarin P_REPLAY_visa for the visa-replay case. Empirical: D4 BASELINE R1_v013_replay pass.

scenarios.title: Signature produced by non-issuer key
scenarios.description: An attacker signs a REQ with a key that does not match the agent_id claimed in from_agent. The receiver should reject based on signature verification failure.
scenarios.notes: Verified by Tamarin P_AUTH lemma. Empirical: D4 BASELINE C5 pass.

scenarios.title: Identity document signature does not verify
scenarios.description: An attacker presents an identity document whose document_signature does not verify against the inception key. The receiver should reject before any cap-handling.
scenarios.notes: Empirical: D4 BASELINE S2 pass + D5 Phase B S2 0 findings.

scenarios.title: REQ missing audit_context or required fields
scenarios.description: An attacker submits a REQ lacking the audit_context field, or with any of the four required keys (purpose, request_id, audience_hint, expires_at) missing/empty.
scenarios.v0_8_behavior: rejected after migration window closes; pre-window: synthesized placeholder + DeprecationWarning
scenarios.notes: Migration semantics in spec §18.7. AIP parallel: aip_token_malformed on missing per-delegation context.

scenarios.title: Visa-use signature replayed as holder_proof (P_BIND attack)
scenarios.description: An attacker observes a Use_Visa signature on the wire (sign(nonce, sk_h)) and submits it as a holder_proof on a REQ where req_id = nonce. In v0.7 the verifier accepts because both signatures are bare-payload over a single bitstring; in v0.8 the domain-separated signed payloads have structurally distinct shapes and the verify check fails.
scenarios.notes: Closure machine-verified by Tamarin Run 3 (spec/models/pact_core_v0_8.spthy). Spec §18.1 defines the v0.8 signed payload structure.

scenarios.title: REQ audit_context.audience_hint != REQ.to_agent
scenarios.description: An attacker submits a REQ where the audit_context claims a different audience than the actual receiver. The receiver should detect the mismatch at parse time.
scenarios.notes: AIP defines AudienceError as a distinct A2A binding error class; PACT v1.4 adopts the vocabulary as pact_audience_mismatch.

scenarios.title: Adversary holds stolen private key, impersonates legitimate holder
scenarios.description: An attacker has obtained the private key of a legitimate holder via out-of-band compromise. The attacker produces valid signatures over arbitrary REQ payloads. The substrate has no defense against this; documented as the KEY-COMP limitation.
scenarios.notes: Documented limitation per spec §18.9 + §20.3. Mitigation: key rotation (§18.5) or revocation. Empirical: D5 Phase B records 0 real findings across 3 LLM adversaries × 5 Gap-B targets — substrate behaves correctly under the design (accepts the stolen-key attempt).

scenarios.title: MITM reorders fields inside cap_envelope or REQ
scenarios.description: An attacker on the wire reorders the JSON keys in cap_envelope or other REQ subfields before delivery to the receiver. PACT's canonical-JSON discipline (§3) sorts keys before signing, so any wire-level reordering does not affect the canonicalized bytes; the signature still verifies.
scenarios.notes: Expected accept-as-noise: canonicalization makes the attack equivalent to no-op. Real finding only if MITM adds an UNKNOWN field (extra_field strategy) that bypasses signature coverage — currently signature covers all fields. Empirical: D5 Phase B MITM × P-BIND records 0 real findings.

scenarios.title: Adversary brute-forces agent_id prefix collision
scenarios.description: An attacker generates many keypairs and finds one whose agent_id matches a target's first N bytes. The lookalike submits a REQ to the target's contacts hoping the receiver compares only prefixes. PACT's full-hash comparison (§4.1) defeats this; receiver rejects on unknown_peer or similar.
scenarios.notes: Verified by SHA-256 collision resistance assumption (§20.4). Empirical: D5 Phase B SYBIL × P-AUTH records 0 real findings; lookalike rejected with fault=unknown_peer in observed runs.

scenarios.title: Malicious intermediate modifies signed receipt payload
scenarios.description: A delegating intermediate party receives a signed receipt and modifies its payload field before forwarding to the audit consumer. Ed25519 signature verification fails because the canonical JSON of the tampered payload no longer matches what was signed.
scenarios.v0_8_behavior: rejected via signature failure + bilateral-receipt check tightens (§18.6)
scenarios.notes: Empirical: D5 Phase B DELEG-MAL × P-AUDIT records 0 real findings. The substrate's signature coverage of payload bytes is what prevents this.

summary.comment: v0.7 had one vulnerability (holder-proof-replay, Tamarin Run 2 Finding #1). v0.8 closes it. The 3 accepted-as-expected scenarios on v0.8 are: key-compromise-impersonation (documented limitation), mitm-canonical-reorder (canonicalization makes it a no-op), and the documented_limitation flagged as 'pact_ok'.

aip_comparison.comment: Direct comparison with the AIP attack scenario catalogue at /tmp/aip-check/site/paper/data/attacks.json.
aip_comparison.structural_difference: AIP's catalogue is a paper-site demo (3 scenarios in site/paper/data/attacks.json). PACT's catalogue is a normative reference shipped with the spec (§18.8), versioned with the spec, every scenario mapped to a formal lemma + a Stage 2 probe.
